items.q: What is the difference between strength and power?
items.correct: Strength is maximum force; power is force times speed of movement.
items.random_wrong: Strength is for the upper body, power for the lower.
items.why: Students conflate strength and power. A strong person can lift heavy slowly; a powerful athlete generates force QUICKLY (sprinter, shot-putter). Power requires both force and velocity.

items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.

items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.

items.q: Static stretches in cool down should be held for:
items.why: It's 20-30 seconds. “1 second” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Days after exercise. “Before warm-up” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: After how many hours can DOMS appear (rough)?
items.why: Exact answer: 24. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
